text:
• Linear regression on the last 10 memory samples to predict when memory will hit 0.
How It Works
Memory (MB)
2000
1500
1000
- threshold (1024 MB)
500
•
→ Time (ticks)
T-9
T-7 T-5 T-3 T-1 T+?
10 samples —
"When does line hit 0?"
The Math (regular_series.rs:109-132)
Step 1: Linear regression on history
// Fit line: y = slope * x + intercept
// Where x = tick index (0-9), у = available_memory_bytes
let (slope, intercept) = linear_regression_of(&points)
Step 2: Solve for when y = target (0 MB)
|/ At what x does the line hit target_value?
x_target = (target_value - intercept) / slope
// How many ticks from NOW until we hit target?
ticks_until_target = x_target - (current_index)
Step 3: Check if within forecast window
// forecast_interval_ms = 5000ms, tick_interval_ms = 1000ms
// → forecast_ticks = 5
// Overload if memory will hit 0 within 0 to 5 ticks
overload = (0.0 <= ticks_until_target) && (ticks_until_target <= 5.0)

Linear regression → slope = -150 MB/tick, intercept = 2000 MB
When does memory hit o?
0 =
-150 * x + 2000
x = 13.3 ticks
Ticks from now = 13.3 - 9 = 4.3 ticks
4.3 < 5 (forecast window) → OVERLOAD DETECTED
Config
Parameter
forecast_interval_ms
tick_interval_ms
→ forecast_ticks
Default
5000 ms
1000 ms
5
Description
How far ahead to look
Sample rate
Forecast window in samples
Translation: "If current memory trend continues, will we run out within 5 seconds?"
